human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for healthcare-aware travel operations execution, lifecycle-state controls, booking and readiness evidence, PHI handling, eligibility/referral/authorization prerequisites, clinical and payer escalation, completion evidence, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required healthcare domain scenario families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared baseline, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to administrative healthcare travel operations execution using patient/member, payer, referral, authorization, encounter, discharge, transport, lodging, broker, CRM, claim, denial, appeal, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover Medicaid transportation assurance, CMS NEMT resources, CMS eligibility and prior authorization, CMS managed care appeals and grievances, HHS OCR PHI handling, CAQH CORE administrative transactions, AHRQ patient/member experience, Joint Commission identity safety, and DOT accessible travel guidance.

Scenario set covers normal execution, missing prerequisites, authorization/referral blockers, PHI-sensitive requests, broker exceptions, clinical and payer boundary cases, in-transit pickup failure, completion without evidence, revenue-cycle handoffs, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and unsupported identity workarounds.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later trusted built transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant, payer, facility, jurisdiction, state Medicaid, broker, supplier, privacy, and clinical differences are handled through retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
